assertion_results.text: Version impact table shows NO (not affected) for all supported versions — every version ships a dependency version outside the CVE's affected range
assertion_results.evidence: In version-impact.md, the Step 2.4 Version Impact Table lists all 7 versions (2.1.0, 2.1.1, 2.2.0, 2.2.1, 2.2.2, 2.2.3, 2.2.4) with Affected? column showing **NO** for every entry. Each version ships serde_json >= 1.0.137, which is above the fix threshold of 1.0.135. The summary states: 'No supported version is affected.'

assertion_results.text: Triage outcome recommends closing as Not a Bug with resolution, not creating remediation tasks (Step 7 Case C)
assertion_results.evidence: In triage-outcome.md, the decision header reads 'Decision: Close as Not a Bug (Not Affected)' with 'Case C — No supported versions are affected.' Step 7 is titled 'Remediation: Case C (Close)' and the Proposed Jira Actions item 2 states: 'Transition TC-8002 to Closed with resolution Not a Bug.' The Remediation Tasks section explicitly states: 'None required. No remediation tasks are created because no supported version is affected (Case C).'

assertion_results.text: The close recommendation includes a comment documenting the version impact evidence — listing each version and its dependency version as proof of non-impact
assertion_results.evidence: In triage-outcome.md, Proposed Jira Actions item 1 ('Add comment') includes a detailed table listing every version with its serde_json dependency version and Affected? status: 2.1.0/1.0.137/NO, 2.1.1/1.0.137/NO, 2.2.0/1.0.138/NO, 2.2.1/1.0.138/NO, 2.2.2/—/NO (retag), 2.2.3/1.0.139/NO, 2.2.4/1.0.139/NO. The comment concludes: 'All supported versions ship serde_json >= 1.0.137, which is outside the affected range (versions before 1.0.135).'

assertion_results.text: VEX Justification is set to 'Component not Present' or appropriate value when the VEX field is configured (Step 7 VEX Justification)
assertion_results.evidence: In triage-outcome.md, Proposed Jira Actions item 3 states: 'Set VEX Justification (customfield_12345) to "Component not Present"'. The field is configured (customfield_12345 per data-extraction.md). The output also discusses 'Vulnerable Code not Present' as an alternative and notes 'The default per skill guidance is "Component not Present" when the vulnerable package version is not included.' Both values mentioned are appropriate VEX justifications.

assertion_results.text: The analysis does NOT create any remediation tasks — no upstream backport, no downstream propagation (Step 7 Case C closes the issue)
assertion_results.evidence: In triage-outcome.md, the Remediation Tasks section states: 'None required. No remediation tasks are created because no supported version is affected (Case C).' The Cross-Stream Impact section states: 'None. Both the 2.1.x and 2.2.x streams are unaffected. No cross-stream notifications or preemptive remediation tasks are needed.' No remediation task creation appears anywhere in any of the three output files.
